title: Management Summary — Architecture Assessment fragment
description: Structured data for `### Architecture Assessment`. The renderer emits a 4-column 'Weakness category | Affected component(s) | Description | Key findings' table. The prose intro + closing link to §7 are fixed by the template — the LLM only supplies the verdict sentence + weakness rows. Legacy `defects[]` shape is auto-upgraded by the renderer; new fragments MUST use `weaknesses[]`.

verdict_severity.description: Drives the leading emoji for the architecture verdict sentence.

verdict_prose.description: Architecture-level verdict sentence(s). Begins with a bold phrase like '**Verdict — …**'. The renderer prepends the severity emoji.

framing.description: Short framing sentence introducing the weaknesses table (e.g. 'Four cross-cutting weaknesses drive the majority of findings:').

weaknesses.description: Cross-cutting architectural weaknesses, grouped by general security domain. Rendered as 4-column table rows.

category.description: General security domain — e.g. 'Cryptography & Secret Management', 'Query Construction and Data Access', 'Authorization and Access Control', 'Output Encoding and Rendering'. NOT a finding restatement or a code-fragment summary.

affected_components.description: Component-ids (or {id, name} dicts) whose code carries this weakness. The renderer enriches bare ids to '[C-NN](#c-nn) — Component Name' using the components index from threat-model.yaml.

description.description: Design-review prose explaining the structural problem — what security boundary is failing, why it spans multiple call-sites, why a single-line patch will not close it. References affected files/components GENERICALLY (no line-by-line SAST listing). Target 2-4 sentences.

findings.description:
Linked findings. Each item is {ref, label}; renderer emits '[ref](#anchor) — label' with
between rows.

defects.description: LEGACY (pre-2026-05) shape. Accepted for back-compat; the renderer maps `defects[]` → `weaknesses[]` on load (aliases `name`→`category`, defaults `affected_components` to `[]`). New fragments MUST use `weaknesses[]`.

closing_ref_anchor.description: Anchor target for the 'See §7' closing line. Defaults to §7.
